542:       def find_some(ids)

568:       def find_some_ordered(ids)
569:         ids = ids.slice(offset_value || 0, limit_value || ids.size) || []

627:           end
628:         end

637:           if relation.order_values.empty? || relation.has_limit_or_offset?

642:         end
643:       end

649:       def ordered_relation

662:               Set at least one of `implicit_order_column`, `query_constraints` or `primary_key` on
663:               the model when no `order `is specified on the relation.

667:               Calling order dependent finder methods (e.g. `#first`, `#second`) without `order` values on the relation,
668:               and on a model (#{model}) that does not have any order columns (`implicit_order_column`, `query_constraints`,

670:               in Rails 8.2.

672: 
673:             self
674:           end
675:         else

677:         end

680:       def _order_columns
681:         columns = Array(model.implicit_order_column)
682: 
683:         return columns.compact if columns.length.positive? && columns.last.nil?

13:     # Merges in the conditions from <tt>other</tt>, if <tt>other</tt> is an ActiveRecord::Relation.
14:     # Returns an array representing the intersection of the resulting records with <tt>other</tt>, if <tt>other</tt> is an array.
15:     #
16:     #   Post.where(published: true).joins(:comments).merge( Comment.where(spam: false) )
17:     #   # Performs a single join query with both where conditions.
18:     #
19:     #   recent_posts = Post.order('created_at DESC').first(5)
20:     #   Post.where(published: true).merge(recent_posts)
21:     #   # Returns the intersection of all published posts with the 5 most recently created posts.
22:     #   # (This is just an example. You'd probably want to do this with a single query!)
23:     #
24:     # Procs will be evaluated by merge:
25:     #
26:     #   Post.where(published: true).merge(-> { joins(:comments) })
27:     #   # => Post.where(published: true).joins(:comments)
28:     #
29:     # This is mainly intended for sharing common conditions between multiple associations.
30:     #
31:     # For conditions that exist in both relations, those from <tt>other</tt> will take precedence.
32:     # To find the intersection of two relations, use QueryMethods#and.
33:     def merge(other, *rest)
34:       if other.is_a?(Array)
35:         records & other
36:       elsif other
37:         spawn.merge!(other, *rest)
38:       else
39:         raise ArgumentError, "invalid argument: #{other.inspect}."
40:       end
41:     end
42: 
43:     def merge!(other, *rest) # :nodoc:
44:       if other.is_a?(Hash)
45:         Relation::HashMerger.new(self, other).merge
46:       elsif other.is_a?(Relation)
47:         Relation::Merger.new(self, other).merge
48:       elsif other.respond_to?(:to_proc)
49:         instance_exec(&other)
50:       else
51:         raise ArgumentError, "#{other.inspect} is not an ActiveRecord::Relation"
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     # Removes the condition(s) specified in +skips+ from the query.
56:     #
57:     #   Post.order('id asc').except(:order)                  # removes the order condition
58:     #   Post.where('id > 10').order('id asc').except(:where) # removes the where condition but keeps the order
59:     def except(*skips)
60:       relation_with values.except(*skips)
61:     end
62: 
63:     # Keeps only the condition(s) specified in +onlies+ in the query, removing all others.
64:     #
65:     #   Post.order('id asc').only(:where)         # keeps only the where condition, removes the order
66:     #   Post.order('id asc').only(:where, :order) # keeps only the where and order conditions
67:     def only(*onlies)
68:       relation_with values.slice(*onlies)
69:     end

71:     private
72:       def relation_with(values)
73:         result = spawn
74:         result.instance_variable_set(:@values, values)
75:         result
76:       end
77:   end

1627:     # Excludes the specified record (or collection of records) from the resulting
1628:     # relation. For example:
1629:     #
1630:     #   Post.excluding(post)
1631:     #   # SELECT "posts".* FROM "posts" WHERE "posts"."id" != 1
1632:     #
1633:     #   Post.excluding(post_one, post_two)
1634:     #   # SELECT "posts".* FROM "posts" WHERE "posts"."id" NOT IN (1, 2)
1635:     #
1636:     #   Post.excluding(Post.drafts)
1637:     #   # SELECT "posts".* FROM "posts" WHERE "posts"."id" NOT IN (3, 4, 5)
1638:     #
1639:     # This can also be called on associations. As with the above example, either
1640:     # a single record of collection thereof may be specified:
1641:     #
1642:     #   post = Post.find(1)
1643:     #   comment = Comment.find(2)
1644:     #   post.comments.excluding(comment)
1645:     #   # SELECT "comments".* FROM "comments" WHERE "comments"."post_id" = 1 AND "comments"."id" != 2
1646:     #
1647:     # This is short-hand for <tt>.where.not(id: post.id)</tt> and <tt>.where.not(id: [post_one.id, post_two.id])</tt>.
1648:     #
1649:     # An <tt>ArgumentError</tt> will be raised if either no records are
1650:     # specified, or if any of the records in the collection (if a collection
1651:     # is passed in) are not instances of the same model that the relation is
1652:     # scoping.
1653:     def excluding(*records)
1654:       relations = records.extract! { |element| element.is_a?(Relation) }
1655:       records.flatten!(1)
1656:       records.compact!
1657: 
1658:       unless records.all?(model) && relations.all? { |relation| relation.model == model }
1659:         raise ArgumentError, "You must only pass a single or collection of #{model.name} objects to ##{__callee__}."
1660:       end
1661: 
1662:       spawn.excluding!(records + relations.flat_map(&:ids))
1663:     end
1664:     alias :without :excluding

1692:       def build_where_clause(opts, rest = []) # :nodoc:
1693:         opts = sanitize_forbidden_attributes(opts)
1694: 
1695:         if opts.is_a?(Array)
1696:           opts, *rest = opts
1697:         end
1698: 
1699:         case opts
1700:         when String
1701:           if rest.empty?
1702:             parts = [Arel.sql(opts)]
1703:           elsif rest.first.is_a?(Hash) && /:\w+/.match?(opts)
1704:             parts = [build_named_bound_sql_literal(opts, rest.first)]
1705:           elsif opts.include?("?")
1706:             parts = [build_bound_sql_literal(opts, rest)]
1707:           else
1708:             parts = [Arel.sql(model.sanitize_sql([opts, *rest]))]
1709:           end
1710:         when Hash
1711:           opts = opts.transform_keys do |key|
1712:             if key.is_a?(Array)
1713:               key.map { |k| model.attribute_aliases[k.to_s] || k.to_s }
1714:             else
1715:               key = key.to_s
1716:               model.attribute_aliases[key] || key
1717:             end
1718:           end
1719:           references = PredicateBuilder.references(opts)
1720:           self.references_values |= references unless references.empty?
1721: 
1722:           parts = predicate_builder.build_from_hash(opts) do |table_name|
1723:             lookup_table_klass_from_join_dependencies(table_name)
1724:           end
1725:         when Arel::Nodes::Node
1726:           parts = [opts]
1727:         else
1728:           raise ArgumentError, "Unsupported argument type: #{opts} (#{opts.class})"
1729:         end
1730: 
1731:         Relation::WhereClause.new(parts)
1732:       end

1760:       def build_named_bound_sql_literal(statement, values)
1761:         bound_values = values.transform_values do |value|
1762:           if ActiveRecord::Relation === value
1763:             Arel.sql(value.to_sql)
1764:           elsif value.respond_to?(:map) && !value.acts_like?(:string)
1765:             values = value.map { |v| v.respond_to?(:id_for_database) ? v.id_for_database : v }
1766:             values.empty? ? nil : values
1767:           else
1768:             value = value.id_for_database if value.respond_to?(:id_for_database)
1769:             value
1770:           end
1771:         end
1772: 
1773:         begin
1774:           Arel::Nodes::BoundSqlLiteral.new("(#{statement})", nil, bound_values)
1775:         rescue Arel::BindError => error
1776:           raise ActiveRecord::PreparedStatementInvalid, error.message
1777:         end
1778:       end
1779: 
1780:       def build_bound_sql_literal(statement, values)
1781:         bound_values = values.map do |value|
1782:           if ActiveRecord::Relation === value
1783:             Arel.sql(value.to_sql)
1784:           elsif value.respond_to?(:map) && !value.acts_like?(:string)
1785:             values = value.map { |v| v.respond_to?(:id_for_database) ? v.id_for_database : v }
1786:             values.empty? ? nil : values
1787:           else
1788:             value = value.id_for_database if value.respond_to?(:id_for_database)
1789:             value
1790:           end
1791:         end
1792: 
1793:         begin
1794:           Arel::Nodes::BoundSqlLiteral.new("(#{statement})", bound_values, nil)
1795:         rescue Arel::BindError => error
1796:           raise ActiveRecord::PreparedStatementInvalid, error.message
1797:         end
1798:       end

1800:       def lookup_table_klass_from_join_dependencies(table_name)

1813:       def build_join_dependencies
1814:         joins = joins_values | left_outer_joins_values
1815:         joins |= eager_load_values unless eager_load_values.empty?
1816:         joins |= includes_values unless includes_values.empty?
1817: 
1818:         join_dependencies = []
1819:         join_dependencies.unshift construct_join_dependency(
1820:           select_named_joins(joins, join_dependencies), nil

1828:       def build_arel(aliases)

1830: 
1831:         build_joins(arel.join_sources, aliases)
1832: 
1833:         arel.where(where_clause.ast) unless where_clause.empty?
1834:         arel.having(having_clause.ast) unless having_clause.empty?
1835:         arel.take(build_cast_value("LIMIT", limit_value)) if limit_value
1836:         arel.skip(build_cast_value("OFFSET", offset_value.to_i)) if offset_value
1837:         arel.group(*arel_columns(group_values)) unless group_values.empty?
1838: 
1839:         build_order(arel)
1840:         build_with(arel)
1841:         build_select(arel)
1842: 
1843:         arel.optimizer_hints(*optimizer_hints_values) unless optimizer_hints_values.empty?
1844:         arel.comment(*annotate_values) unless annotate_values.empty?
1845:         arel.distinct(distinct_value)
1846:         arel.from(build_from) unless from_clause.empty?
1847:         arel.lock(lock_value) if lock_value
1848: 
1849:         arel

1856:       def build_from
1857:         opts = from_clause.value
1858:         name = from_clause.name&.to_s || "subquery"
1859:         case opts

1861:           if opts.eager_loading?

1863:           end
1864:           opts.arel.as(name)
1865:         when Arel::Nodes::Union, Arel::Nodes::UnionAll,
1866:              Arel::Nodes::Intersect, Arel::Nodes::Except
1867:           opts.as(name)

1873:       def select_named_joins(join_names, stashed_joins = nil, &block)
1874:         cte_joins, associations = join_names.partition do |join_name|
1875:           Symbol === join_name && with_values.any? { _1.key?(join_name) }
1876:         end
1877: 
1878:         cte_joins.each do |cte_name|
1879:           block&.call(CTEJoin.new(cte_name))
1880:         end
1881: 
1882:         select_association_list(associations, stashed_joins, &block)
1883:       end

1885:       def select_association_list(associations, stashed_joins = nil)
1886:         result = []
1887:         associations.each do |association|
1888:           case association
1889:           when Hash, Symbol, Array
1890:             result << association
1891:           when ActiveRecord::Associations::JoinDependency
1892:             stashed_joins&.<< association
1893:           else
1894:             yield association if block_given?
1895:           end
1896:         end
1897:         result
1898:       end

1900:       def build_join_buckets
1901:         buckets = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
1902: 
1903:         unless left_outer_joins_values.empty?
1904:           stashed_left_joins = []
1905:           left_joins = select_named_joins(left_outer_joins_values, stashed_left_joins) do |left_join|
1906:             if left_join.is_a?(CTEJoin)
1907:               buckets[:join_node] << build_with_join_node(left_join.name, Arel::Nodes::OuterJoin)
1908:             else
1909:               raise ArgumentError, "only Hash, Symbol and Array are allowed"
1910:             end
1911:           end
1912: 
1913:           if joins_values.empty?
1914:             buckets[:named_join] = left_joins
1915:             buckets[:stashed_join] = stashed_left_joins
1916:             return buckets, Arel::Nodes::OuterJoin
1917:           else
1918:             stashed_left_joins.unshift construct_join_dependency(left_joins, Arel::Nodes::OuterJoin)
1919:           end
1920:         end
1921: 
1922:         joins = joins_values.dup
1923:         if joins.last.is_a?(ActiveRecord::Associations::JoinDependency)
1924:           stashed_eager_load = joins.pop if joins.last.base_klass == model
1925:         end
1926: 
1927:         joins.each_with_index do |join, i|
1928:           joins[i] = Arel::Nodes::StringJoin.new(Arel.sql(join.strip)) if join.is_a?(String)
1929:         end
1930: 
1931:         while joins.first.is_a?(Arel::Nodes::Join)
1932:           join_node = joins.shift
1933:           if !join_node.is_a?(Arel::Nodes::LeadingJoin) && (stashed_eager_load || stashed_left_joins)
1934:             buckets[:join_node] << join_node
1935:           else
1936:             buckets[:leading_join] << join_node
1937:           end
1938:         end
1939: 
1940:         buckets[:named_join] = select_named_joins(joins, buckets[:stashed_join]) do |join|
1941:           if join.is_a?(Arel::Nodes::Join)
1942:             buckets[:join_node] << join
1943:           elsif join.is_a?(CTEJoin)
1944:             buckets[:join_node] << build_with_join_node(join.name)
1945:           else
1946:             raise "unknown class: %s" % join.class.name
1947:           end
1948:         end
1949: 
1950:         buckets[:stashed_join].concat stashed_left_joins if stashed_left_joins
1951:         buckets[:stashed_join] << stashed_eager_load if stashed_eager_load
1952: 
1953:         return buckets, Arel::Nodes::InnerJoin
1954:       end

1956:       def build_joins(join_sources, aliases = nil)
1957:         return join_sources if joins_values.empty? && left_outer_joins_values.empty?
1958: 
1959:         buckets, join_type = build_join_buckets
1960: 
1961:         named_joins   = buckets[:named_join]
1962:         stashed_joins = buckets[:stashed_join]
1963:         leading_joins = buckets[:leading_join]
1964:         join_nodes    = buckets[:join_node]
1965: 
1966:         join_sources.concat(leading_joins) unless leading_joins.empty?
1967: 
1968:         unless named_joins.empty? && stashed_joins.empty?
1969:           alias_tracker = alias_tracker(leading_joins + join_nodes, aliases)
1970:           join_dependency = construct_join_dependency(named_joins, join_type)
1971:           join_sources.concat(join_dependency.join_constraints(stashed_joins, alias_tracker, references_values))
1972:         end
1973: 
1974:         join_sources.concat(join_nodes) unless join_nodes.empty?
1975:         join_sources
1976:       end

1981:         elsif model.ignored_columns.any? || model.enumerate_columns_in_select_statements || model.only_columns.any?

1983:         else
1984:           arel.project(table[Arel.star])

1990: 
1991:         with_statements = with_values.map do |with_value|
1992:           build_with_value_from_hash(with_value)
1993:         end
1994: 
1995:         @with_is_recursive ? arel.with(:recursive, with_statements) : arel.with(with_statements)

1998:       def build_with_value_from_hash(hash)
1999:         hash.map do |name, value|
2000:           Arel::Nodes::TableAlias.new(build_with_expression_from_value(value), name)
2001:         end

2004:       def build_with_expression_from_value(value, nested = false)
2005:         case value
2006:         when Arel::Nodes::SqlLiteral, Arel::Nodes::BoundSqlLiteral
2007:           Arel::Nodes::Grouping.new(value)
2008:         when ActiveRecord::Relation
2009:           if nested
2010:             value.arel.ast
2011:           else
2012:             value.arel
2013:           end
2014:         when Arel::SelectManager then value
2015:         when Array
2016:           return build_with_expression_from_value(value.first, false) if value.size == 1
2017: 
2018:           parts = value.map do |query|
2019:             build_with_expression_from_value(query, true)
2020:           end
2021: 
2022:           parts.reduce do |result, value|
2023:             Arel::Nodes::UnionAll.new(result, value)
2024:           end
2025:         else
2026:           raise ArgumentError, "Unsupported argument type: `#{value}` #{value.class}"
2027:         end
2028:       end

2032: 
2033:         table.join(with_table, kind).on(
2034:           with_table[model.model_name.to_s.foreign_key].eq(table[model.primary_key])
2035:         ).join_sources.first

2038:       def arel_columns_from_hash(fields)
2039:         fields.flat_map do |table_name, columns|
2040:           table_name = table_name.name if table_name.is_a?(Symbol)
2041:           case columns
2042:           when Symbol, String
2043:             arel_column_with_table(table_name, columns)
2044:           when Array
2045:             columns.map do |column|
2046:               arel_column_with_table(table_name, column)
2047:             end
2048:           else
2049:             raise TypeError, "Expected Symbol, String or Array, got: #{columns.class}"
2050:           end
2051:         end
2052:       end

2054:       def arel_column_with_table(table_name, column_name)
2055:         self.references_values |= [Arel.sql(table_name, retryable: true)]
2056: 
2057:         if column_name.is_a?(Symbol) || !column_name.match?(/\W/)
2058:           predicate_builder.resolve_arel_attribute(table_name, column_name) do
2059:             lookup_table_klass_from_join_dependencies(table_name)
2060:           end
2061:         else
2062:           Arel.sql("#{model.adapter_class.quote_table_name(table_name)}.#{column_name}")
2063:         end
2064:       end
2065: 
2066:       def arel_column(field)
2067:         field = field.name if is_symbol = field.is_a?(Symbol)
2068: 
2069:         field = model.attribute_aliases[field] || field
2070:         from = from_clause.name || from_clause.value
2071: 
2072:         if model.columns_hash.key?(field) && (!from || table_name_matches?(from))
2073:           table[field]
2074:         elsif /\A(?<table>(?:\w+\.)?\w+)\.(?<column>\w+)\z/ =~ field
2075:           arel_column_with_table(table, column)
2076:         elsif block_given?
2077:           yield field
2078:         elsif Arel.arel_node?(field)
2079:           field
2080:         elsif is_symbol
2081:           Arel.sql(model.adapter_class.quote_table_name(field), retryable: true)
2082:         else
2083:           Arel.sql(field)
2084:         end
2085:       end
2086: 
2087:       def table_name_matches?(from)
2088:         table_name = Regexp.escape(table.name)
2089:         quoted_table_name = Regexp.escape(model.adapter_class.quote_table_name(table.name))
2090:         /(?:\A|(?<!FROM)\s)(?:\b#{table_name}\b|#{quoted_table_name})(?!\.)/i.match?(from.to_s)
2091:       end

2093:       def reverse_sql_order(order_query)
2094:         if order_query.empty?
2095:           if !_reverse_order_columns.empty?
2096:             return _reverse_order_columns.map { |column| table[column].desc }
2097:           end
2098: 
2099:           raise IrreversibleOrderError, <<~MSG.squish
2100:             Relation has no order values, and #{model} has no order columns to use as a default.
2101:             Set at least one of `implicit_order_column`, or `primary_key` on the model when no
2102:             `order `is specified on the relation.
2103:           MSG
2104:         end
2105: 
2106:         order_query.flat_map do |o|
2107:           case o
2108:           when Arel::Attribute
2109:             o.desc
2110:           when Arel::Nodes::Ordering
2111:             o.reverse
2112:           when Arel::Nodes::NodeExpression
2113:             o.desc
2114:           when String
2115:             if does_not_support_reverse?(o)
2116:               raise IrreversibleOrderError, "Order #{o.inspect} cannot be reversed automatically"
2117:             end
2118:             o.split(",").map! do |s|
2119:               s.strip!
2120:               s.gsub!(/\sasc\Z/i, " DESC") || s.gsub!(/\sdesc\Z/i, " ASC") || (s << " DESC")
2121:             end
2122:           else
2123:             o
2124:           end
2125:         end
2126:       end

2128:       def _reverse_order_columns
2129:         roc = []
2130:         roc << model.implicit_order_column if model.implicit_order_column

418:       def aggregate_column(column_name)

420:         when Arel::Expressions

422:         when :all

438:       def perform_calculation(operation, column_name)
439:         operation = operation.to_s.downcase
440: 
441:         # If #count is used with #distinct (i.e. `relation.distinct.count`) it is
442:         # considered distinct.
443:         distinct = distinct_value
444: 
445:         if operation == "count"
446:           column_name ||= select_for_count
447:           if column_name == :all
448:             if !distinct
449:               distinct = distinct_select?(select_for_count) if group_values.empty?
450:             elsif group_values.any? || select_values.empty? && order_values.empty?
451:               column_name = primary_key
452:             end
453:           elsif distinct_select?(column_name)

456:         end
457: 
458:         if group_values.any?
459:           execute_grouped_calculation(operation, column_name, distinct)
460:         else
461:           execute_simple_calculation(operation, column_name, distinct)

465:       def distinct_select?(column_name)

469:       def possible_aggregation?(column_names)

471:           if column_name.is_a?(String)
472:             column_name.include?("(")
473:           else
474:             Arel.arel_node?(column_name)

477:       end

479:       def operation_over_aggregate_column(column, operation, distinct)
480:         operation == "count" ? column.count(distinct) : column.public_send(operation)

483:       def execute_simple_calculation(operation, column_name, distinct) # :nodoc:
484:         if build_count_subquery?(operation, column_name, distinct)
485:           # Shortcut when limit is zero.
486:           return @async ? Promise::Complete.new(0) : 0 if limit_value == 0

488:           relation = self
489:           query_builder = build_count_subquery(spawn, column_name, distinct)
490:         else
491:           # PostgreSQL doesn't like ORDER BY when there are no GROUP BY
492:           relation = unscope(:order).distinct!(false)
493: 
494:           column = relation.aggregate_column(column_name)
495:           select_value = operation_over_aggregate_column(column, operation, distinct)
496:           select_value.distinct = true if operation == "sum" && distinct

498:           relation.select_values = [select_value]

500:           query_builder = relation.arel
501:         end
502: 
503:         query_result = if relation.where_clause.contradiction?
504:           if @async
505:             FutureResult.wrap(ActiveRecord::Result.empty)
506:           else
507:             ActiveRecord::Result.empty
508:           end

510:           skip_query_cache_if_necessary do
511:             model.with_connection do |c|
512:               c.select_all(query_builder, "#{model.name} #{operation.capitalize}", async: @async)

514:           end

528:       def execute_grouped_calculation(operation, column_name, distinct) # :nodoc:
529:         group_fields = group_values
530: 
531:         if group_fields.size == 1 && group_fields.first.respond_to?(:to_sym)
532:           association  = model._reflect_on_association(group_fields.first)
533:           associated   = association && association.belongs_to? # only count belongs_to associations
534:           group_fields = Array(association.foreign_key) if associated
535:         end
536: 
537:         relation = except(:group).distinct!(false)
538:         group_fields = relation.arel_columns(group_fields)

540:         model.with_connection do |connection|
541:           column_alias_tracker = ColumnAliasTracker.new(connection)

543:           group_aliases = group_fields.map { |field|
544:             field = connection.visitor.compile(field) if Arel.arel_node?(field)
545:             column_alias_tracker.alias_for(field.to_s.downcase)

547:           group_columns = group_aliases.zip(group_fields)
548: 
549:           column = relation.aggregate_column(column_name)
550:           column_alias = column_alias_tracker.alias_for("#{operation} #{column_name.to_s.downcase}")
551:           select_value = operation_over_aggregate_column(column, operation, distinct)
552:           select_value = select_value.as(model.adapter_class.quote_column_name(column_alias))
553: 
554:           select_values = [select_value]
555:           select_values += self.select_values unless having_clause.empty?
556: 
557:           select_values.concat group_columns.map { |aliaz, field|
558:             aliaz = model.adapter_class.quote_column_name(aliaz)
559:             if field.respond_to?(:as)
560:               field.as(aliaz)
561:             else
562:               "#{field} AS #{aliaz}"
563:             end
564:           }
565: 
566:           relation.group_values  = group_fields
567:           relation.select_values = select_values
568: 
569:           result = skip_query_cache_if_necessary do
570:             connection.select_all(relation.arel, "#{model.name} #{operation.capitalize}", async: @async)
571:           end
572: 
573:           result.then do |calculated_data|
574:             if association
575:               key_ids = if association.klass.base_class.composite_primary_key?
576:                 calculated_data.collect { |row| group_aliases.map { |group_alias| row[group_alias] } }
577:               else
578:                 calculated_data.collect { |row| row[group_aliases.first] }
579:               end
580:               key_records = association.klass.base_class.where(association.klass.base_class.primary_key => key_ids)
581:               key_records = key_records.index_by(&:id)
582:             end
583: 
584:             key_types = group_columns.each_with_object({}) do |(aliaz, col_name), types|
585:               types[aliaz] = col_name.try(:type_caster) ||
586:                 type_for(col_name) do
587:                   calculated_data.column_types.fetch(aliaz, Type.default_value)
588:                 end
589:             end
590: 
591:             hash_rows = calculated_data.cast_values(key_types).map! do |row|
592:               calculated_data.columns.each_with_object({}).with_index do |(col_name, hash), i|
593:                 hash[col_name] = row[i]
594:               end
595:             end
596: 
597:             if operation != "count"
598:               type = column.try(:type_caster) ||
599:                 lookup_cast_type_from_join_dependencies(column_name.to_s) || Type.default_value
600:               type = type.subtype if Enum::EnumType === type
601:             end
602: 
603:             hash_rows.each_with_object({}) do |row, result|
604:               key = group_aliases.map { |aliaz| row[aliaz] }
605:               key = key.first if key.size == 1
606:               key = key_records[key] if associated
607: 
608:               result[key] = type_cast_calculated_value(row[column_alias], operation, type)
609:             end
610:           end
611:         end
612:       end
613: 
614:       def type_for(field, &block)
615:         field_name = field.respond_to?(:name) ? field.name.to_s : field.to_s.split(".").last
616:         model.type_for_attribute(field_name, &block)
617:       end
618: 
619:       def lookup_cast_type_from_join_dependencies(name, join_dependencies = build_join_dependencies)
620:         each_join_dependencies(join_dependencies) do |join|
621:           type = join.base_klass.attribute_types.fetch(name, nil)
622:           return type if type
623:         end
624:         nil
625:       end
626: 
627:       def type_cast_pluck_values(result, columns)
628:         cast_types = if result.columns.size != columns.size
629:           model.attribute_types
630:         else
631:           join_dependencies = nil
632:           columns.map.with_index do |column, i|
633:             column.try(:type_caster) ||
634:               model.attribute_types.fetch(name = result.columns[i]) do
635:                 join_dependencies ||= build_join_dependencies
636:                 lookup_cast_type_from_join_dependencies(name, join_dependencies) ||
637:                   result.column_types[i] || Type.default_value
638:               end
639:           end
640:         end
641:         result.cast_values(cast_types)
642:       end
643: 
644:       def type_cast_calculated_value(value, operation, type)
645:         case operation
646:         when "count"
647:           value.to_i
648:         when "sum"
649:           type.deserialize(value || 0)
650:         when "average"
651:           case type.type
652:           when :integer, :decimal
653:             value&.to_d
654:           else
655:             type.deserialize(value)
656:           end
657:         else # "minimum", "maximum"
658:           type.deserialize(value)
659:         end
660:       end
661: 
662:       def select_for_count
663:         if select_values.empty?
664:           :all
665:         else
666:           with_connection do |conn|
667:             arel_columns(select_values).map { |column| conn.visitor.compile(column) }.join(", ")
668:           end
669:         end
670:       end
671: 
672:       def build_count_subquery?(operation, column_name, distinct)
673:         # SQLite and older MySQL does not support `COUNT DISTINCT` with `*` or
674:         # multiple columns, so we need to use subquery for this.
675:         operation == "count" &&
676:           (((column_name == :all || select_values.many?) && distinct) || has_limit_or_offset?)
677:       end
678: 
679:       def build_count_subquery(relation, column_name, distinct)
680:         if column_name == :all
681:           column_alias = Arel.star
682:           relation.select_values = [ Arel.sql(FinderMethods::ONE_AS_ONE) ] unless distinct
683:           relation.unscope!(:order)
684:         else
685:           column_alias = Arel.sql("count_column")
686:           relation.select_values = [ relation.aggregate_column(column_name).as(column_alias) ]
687:         end
688: 
689:         subquery_alias = Arel.sql("subquery_for_count", retryable: true)
690:         select_value = operation_over_aggregate_column(column_alias, "count", false)
691: 
692:         relation.build_subquery(subquery_alias, select_value)
693:       end
694:   end
695: end

414:       # This is a custom implementation of `<=>` operator,

422:           return comparison if comparison != 0

425:         0

441:             values_last = values.last

content.text: Now let me understand the complete picture by examining the key files and their relationships:

## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

This is the Ruby on Rails codebase, specifically the ActiveRecord component which provides ORM functionality. The code is organized as a monorepo with multiple independent components.

**Key structure:**
- `activerecord/` - The ActiveRecord ORM component
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb` - The main Relation class (lines 1-1539)
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/*.rb` - Relation modules (FinderMethods, SpawnMethods, QueryMethods, Calculations, Batches, Delegation)
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/association_relation.rb` - AssociationRelation subclass
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/` - Association implementation

**The lazy query object (Relation):**
The Relation class at `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:4-1539` is the lazy query object that:
1. Gets returned by finders, scopes, and associations
2. Accumulates query conditions as it's chained
3. Gets executed when iteration or specific methods are called

## Step 2: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

**Relation class location:** `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:4-1539`

**What Relation exposes:**
- Core query methods: `where`, `select`, `joins`, `includes`, `order`, `group`, `having`, `limit`, `offset`
- Finder methods: `find`, `find_by`, `take`, `first`, `last`, `exists?`
- Calculations: `count`, `average`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `sum`, `calculate`
- Batch processing: `find_each`, `find_in_batches`, `in_batches`
- Execution: `load`, `to_a`, `each` (triggers query execution)
- Spawning: `merge`, `except`, `only`, `unscope`
- Delegation: Methods delegated to the model

## Step 3: Trace how a query is built up and executed

**Query creation and chaining:**
1. `Model.all` calls `relation` method (`activerecord/lib/active_record/core.rb:442`)
2. This creates a new `Relation` instance via `Relation.create(self)`
3. Chaining methods like `where`, `order` are defined in `QueryMethods` module
4. Each chaining method calls `spawn` to create a new Relation instance with updated values
5. `spawn` is defined in `SpawnMethods` and creates a clone of the relation

**Query execution flow:**
1. When execution is triggered (e.g., `to_a`, `each`, `load`), `exec_queries` (line 1436) is called
2. `exec_queries` calls `exec_main_query` (line 1460) which determines the execution path
3. For eager loading: `apply_join_dependency` builds the join structure
4. For regular queries: `model._query_by_sql` executes the SQL
5. Results are processed in `instantiate_records` (line 1492)
6. Records are type-cast and frozen, then returned

**Key execution methods:**
- `exec_queries` (line 1436) - Main execution entry point
- `exec_main_query` (line 1460) - Determines execution strategy
- `instantiate_records` (line 1492) - Converts DB results to model instances
- `to_sql` (line 1243) - Generates SQL from the relation
- `build_arel` (line 1828) - Builds Arel AST for the query
